mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding real economic activity and higher corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between INDPRO changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is not significantly greater than zero (p<0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals labor market weakness and reduced consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0% are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns with frequency greater than 50% or if the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is elevated (YoY above 3%) and the Fed funds rate remains below the inflation rate, real interest rates turn negative, encouraging investors to shift into equities, boosting 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% and FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL YoY
failure_condition: If the joint condition does not yield an average positive 20-day SPX return that is statistically different from zero (one-sided t-test p>0.05) or if the interaction effect is not positive in a regression.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, each increase in the Fed funds rate raises discount rates on equity cash flows, compressing valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in periods classified as tightening cycles (based on consecutive FEDFUNDS increases), the average 20-day SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand, which prompts negative short-term S&P 500 returns as markets reprice growth expectations.
failure_condition: If events with RSAFS monthly decline >1% are not followed by significantly negative 5-day SPX returns (t-test p<0.05) or if the hit rate is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: In a full employment economy (UNRATE below 4%), further declines in unemployment signal labor market overheating that often precedes monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 days.
failure_condition: If in periods with UNRATE below 4%, further UNRATE declines are not associated with negative 20-day SPX returns (cross-sectional rank IC negative and significant).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in both housing starts (HOUST) and real retail sales (RSAFS) provide a powerful signal of broad demand contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as recession fears intensify.
candidate_trigger: Both HOUST and RSAFS show sequential month-over-month declines
failure_condition: If the joint signal does not predict negative 60-day SPX returns with an average return significantly below zero (p<0.05) or if its Sharpe ratio is lower than a random strategy.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed funds rate rises above 2.5%, it enters a range that historically constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day SPX return after FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.5% is not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p>0.05) or if the hit rate of negative returns is below 60%.
